headline: NYC adopts “City of Yes for Housing Opportunity” citywide rezoning
summary: The City Council approved a citywide zoning text amendment intended to add housing in every neighborhood. It loosens density limits, trims parking mandates, and makes it easier to add accessory units and convert offices.
why_it_matters: It opens incremental new housing across all neighborhoods rather than concentrating it in a few rezoned areas.

headline: California exempts most urban infill housing from CEQA review
summary: State budget legislation removed many infill housing projects from California Environmental Quality Act review. Supporters call it the biggest housing-process reform in decades; critics worry about lost environmental scrutiny.
why_it_matters: CEQA review and litigation have been a leading cause of delay for city housing, so the carve-out can meaningfully shorten timelines in places like San Francisco.

headline: Massachusetts keeps enforcing multifamily zoning near transit
summary: The MBTA Communities law requires 177 transit-served municipalities to zone for multifamily housing as of right near stations. Compliance, deadlines, and legal challenges remain active across the region.
why_it_matters: It forces transit-rich land to allow apartments by right, which is exactly the kind of by-right capacity that speeds projects.

headline: Washington legalizes “middle housing” statewide
summary: House Bill 1110 requires most Washington cities, including Seattle, to allow two to four homes on lots once reserved for single-family houses, and more near frequent transit. Cities are updating their codes to comply.
why_it_matters: It effectively ends exclusive single-family zoning across much of the state, expanding what you can build on a typical residential lot.

headline: Boston moves to allow accessory dwelling units more broadly
summary: Boston has been advancing zoning changes to permit accessory dwelling units in more of the city, part of the administration's push to add gentle-density housing on existing lots.
why_it_matters: ADUs add homes without new land, and broader by-right allowance reduces the need for discretionary relief.

headline: Chicago's Connected Communities ordinance eases transit-oriented housing
summary: The ordinance reduces or removes parking minimums and supports added density near rail and bus corridors, with affordability provisions attached.
why_it_matters: Cutting parking mandates near transit lowers the cost and footprint of new housing where it's most useful.
